segments.text: How often should you post YouTube shorts
in 2026? Here's the answer. YouTube's
algorithm right now is looking for four
things. How many views you get, how many
people [music] watch to the end, how
they engage, and whether they actually
enjoyed it. More posts means more data,
and more data means you figure out what
works faster. [music] Posting every day
seems like the move until you burn out
and disappear for 2 weeks.
That inconsistency will kill your
momentum faster than a slow posting
schedule ever would. But posting once a
week isn't enough either. You're not
giving the algorithm enough content to
push and you're not improving fast
enough. 3 to five shorts per week
[music] is the sweet spot. It keeps you
consistent, gives YouTube enough to work
with, and is actually sustainable long
term. Find your number in that range and
protect it. Show up every [music] week
and the algorithm will start showing up
for you. Subscribe if this helped.
